# y2clerk

## Overview

`orderlabel` is the second package in the Y2 Analytics series. Many of
its functions run heavily on the frequencies data frame created by the
`freqs()` function from the `y2clerk` package. The premier function in
this package is
[`order_label()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_label.md),
but we will quickly go over a few of the other major functions as well.

- [`order_label()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_label.md) -
  takes a frequency table created by `freqs()` and orders it for
  charting. There are several key arguments within the function that
  allow to user to easily specify how they envision the ordering or what
  type of chart the table will be used in. For example, some key
  ordering arguments are “inherent_order_label”, “label_specific”,
  “group_var”, and “stacked”, among others.
- [`order_same()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_same.md) -
  similar to order_label, only this time you give the function another
  data frame to reference, and your new data frame will be ordered in
  the same way.
- [`taking_names()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/taking_names.md) -
  a short function that should be run at the beginning of every project.
  It takes one argument, a data frame, and it will create a new data
  frame of variable names and labels for quick reference and using
  ctrl + F to search for variables.
- [`other_rm()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/other_rm.md) -
  after running freqs, add on `other_rm` to remove all parenthetical
  additions to a variable label as well as “please specify” parts of
  “Other, please specify” type answers. Trims down your labels quickly.
- [`preamble_rm()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/preamble_rm.md) -
  works similar to other_rm. This time it removes question preambles to
  variable labels such as “What is your favorite color? - Blue”, when
  all you want is “Blue”.
- There are other functions too, so be sure to check those out on your
  own!

## Installation

You can install the released version of orderlabel from
[CRAN](https://CRAN.R-project.org) with:

``` r
install.packages("orderlabel")
```

And the development version from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("nick-moffitt/orderlabel")
```

## Examples

Below you will find a few basic examples which show you how to quickly
order your frequencies table with
[`order_label()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_label.md):

``` r
library(orderlabel)
library(dplyr)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'dplyr'
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
#> 
#>     filter, lag
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
#> 
#>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
# Ungrouped, put in descending order of the result
frequencies <- tibble(
  label = c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'),
  result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),
  value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
) %>% order_label()

# # Grouped, with an inherent order for the label, or the brand
# frequencies <- tibble(
#   label = rep(c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'), 2),
#   result = c(.20, .20, .30, .10, .20, .20, .30, .20, .20, .10),
#   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 2),
#   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 5), rep('Group B', 5))
# ) %>% order_label(
#   group_var = group_var,
#   inherent_order_label = T
# )
# 
# # Stacked, will be using this frequencies in an mschart later on
# frequencies <- tibble(
#   label = rep(c('Promoter', 'Passive', 'Detractor'), 3),
#   result = c(.33, .33, .34, .20, .30, .50, .25, .50, .25),
#   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3), 3),
#   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 3), rep('Group B', 3), rep('Group C', 3))
# ) %>% order_label(
#   group_var = group_var,
#   stacked = 'ms'
# )
```

## Help

If you have issues using orderlabel, please post your issue on
[GitHub](https://github.com/nick-moffitt/orderlabel/issues) along with a
minimal reproducible example. We will do our best to address your issues
and get them fixed for the next version of orderlabel.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`add_ages()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/add_ages.md)
  : Add age and census age groups to a data frame
- [`add_regions()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/add_regions.md)
  : Add a "census_region" variable based on states
- [`order_label()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_label.md)
  : Order your data and add percent labels
- [`order_same()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_same.md)
  : Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table
- [`order_topline()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/order_topline.md)
  : Add %s to a topline report
- [`other_rm()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/other_rm.md)
  : Auto change "Other please specify"s into "Other"
- [`preamble_rm()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/preamble_rm.md)
  : Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions
- [`read_excel_allsheets()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/read_excel_allsheets.md)
  : Read multiple excel sheets at once
- [`taking_names()`](https://y2analytics.github.io/orderlabel/reference/taking_names.md)
  : Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels
